Ahmed Latif Moussaoui

Mechanical Engineer | Robotics & Automation

I'm a mechanical engineering grad from Oregon State who builds robots and the software that runs them. I'm drawn to problems that are supposed to be hard, where the fun is figuring out how something works and then getting good at it.

The projects below are what I've been making.

Projects Skills Contact Me
Open to full-time & internship roles — June 2026

More About Me

Projects

Material Sample Testing Automation

ROS2 MoveIt2 Python OpenCV Gazebo / Ignition Docker Raspberry Pi Pick & Place ArUco / Pose Estimation

Automated material sample testing in a Civil Engineering research lab at OSU using a Kinova Gen3 robotic arm, ROS2, and computer vision — from simulation to working prototype.

Autonomous RC Aircraft – DBF Capstone

ArduPilot Mission Planner Avionics Integration Soldering Wiring & Electrical Assembly Autonomous Systems Systems Integration Flight Testing

Led the autonomous systems sub-team for Oregon State's Design Build Fly capstone, achieving the team's first successful autonomous test flight using an ArduPilot-based autopilot.

Emotion Display Robot Face – SAMI

ROS2 Python Raspberry Pi Pygame Embedded Systems Systems Integration

Built the animated LCD face and ROS2 emotion-state interface for SAMI, a servo-actuated humanoid robot developed by the BAJAN Robotics team in ROB 421 Applied Robotics at Oregon State University.

Automated Security Door System

Arduino C++ Embedded Systems Sensor Integration State Machine Design 3D Printing Electronics Breadboarding

A 3D printed miniature automated double-door system with motion detection, a handmade metal detector coil, jam detection, and a full state-machine-driven control system built on Arduino.

Popsicle-Stick Ferris Wheel Build Kits

Fusion 360 Parametric Modeling 3D Printing PrusaSlicer Design for Manufacturing Trigonometry / Geometric Design Batch Production

Designed and 3D printed a 35-kit batch of snap-together joints that let kids build their own working popsicle-stick Ferris wheel, commissioned by a local child development center.

pH Dosing Controller

Raspberry Pi Python Flask REST APIs I2C / Hardware Drivers Control Systems Atlas Scientific EZO Sensors Tailscale VPN / Remote Access

Built an automated pH monitoring and dosing system using a Raspberry Pi 4, Atlas Scientific EZO-pH sensor, and dual EZO-PMP peristaltic pumps to hold a liquid at a target pH setpoint.
